text:
**1. [Blocker ‚Äî Phase E] The Trellis redaction hook leaks resource keys through the audit log.** `redact_trace` (crates/trellis-testing/src/scenario.rs:280-292) redacts `resource_commands`, `resource_coalescences`, and invariant names ‚Äî but not `trace.audit_log`, and `AuditEvent::ResourceOpenCoalesced` carries a full `ResourceKey` (crates/trellis-core/src/transaction_types.rs:87-94). The epic cites these hooks as the redaction foundation; as implemented, a "shareable" capsule would ship un-redacted keys. Suggested change: add a Phase E checklist item ‚Äî "upstream trellis fix: `redact_trace` must cover `audit_log` (and any future key-carrying fields); add a test that serializes a redacted capsule and asserts no original key material survives anywhere in the JSON." The "all prerequisites closed" claim is true of the listed issues, but this gap is new. [...] Assistant: Finding 1 confirmed: `redact_trace` covers `resource_commands`, `resource_coalescences`, and `invariant_results` ‚Äî the `audit_log` is untouched. Checking the audit event and versioning claims: [...] 1. **Trellis's redaction hook leaks resource keys.** `redact_trace` covers resource commands, coalescences, and invariant names ‚Äî but the audit log is cloned into every trace un-redacted, and `AuditEvent::ResourceOpenCoalesced` carries a full `ResourceKey`. A "shareable" capsule built on those hooks would ship the very keys you thought you'd scrubbed. Filed as [trellis#161](https://github.com/pablof7z/trellis/issues/161), now a hard gate for Phase E. [...] Assistant: Two things there ‚Äî the Fable findings are already folded into the epic (done last turn, and I see on the fabric that @codex4 already picked up trellis#161 and has a draft PR, trellis#162). Now let me check the NIP-60/61 situation ‚Äî searching the trackers and code for any existing wallet/nutzap work.

Assistant: Done on both fronts. Here's where things stand and what to hand a coding agent.

**Fable findings: already folded.** Epic #2858 was revised last round with all verified findings, trellis#161 was filed for the redaction leak ‚Äî and I can see on the fabric that @codex4 already fixed it (draft PR [trellis#162](https://github.com/pablof7z/trellis/pull/162), awaiting review/merge). That upstream gate is nearly cleared.
